assertions.evidence: PI_API=openai found in .env for openai provider. PI_API=anthropic found for anthropic provider.
assertions.details: Provider type correctly mapped to PI_API env var.

assertions.evidence: PI_MODEL=gpt-4 found in .env when source has model.model='gpt-4'.
assertions.details: Model name correctly extracted and written to .env.

assertions.evidence: OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-test-key-12345 found in .env when running with --migrate-secrets.
assertions.details: API key migration works correctly with --migrate-secrets flag.

assertions.evidence: No API key found in .env when running without --migrate-secrets. Report shows: model-secrets skipped - 'API keys not migrated (use --migrate-secrets): API_KEY'.
assertions.details: API keys correctly excluded when flag not set.

assertions.evidence: OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://custom.api.example.com/v1 found in .env when source has model.baseUrl.
assertions.details: Custom base URL correctly extracted and written.

assertions.evidence: When .env pre-existing PI_MODEL=existing-model exists and migration runs without --overwrite, the original line is preserved BUT a duplicate PI_MODEL=gpt-4-new is appended. Result: .env has TWO PI_MODEL lines. Same bug as VAL-CHAN-005.
assertions.details: Root cause: In updateEnvFile(), when !this.args.overwrite, existing lines are kept but key is NOT tracked in updatedKeys, causing duplicates. See VAL-CHAN-005 for fix details.

blockers.description: Same updateEnvFile() bug as VAL-CHAN-005. Duplicate keys appended when --overwrite not set and key already exists in .env.
blockers.suggestedFix: Same fix as VAL-CHAN-005: add updatedKeys.add(key) in the else branch of updateEnvFile().
